summary:
Fluoride replaces the hydroxyl ion in hydroxyapatite, resulting in fluorapatite with higher resistance to acid dissolution and improved mechanical strength. Calcium or phosphate substitutions lack support from evidence regarding strengthened crystalline integrity. \boxed{A}  

(Note: The summary strictly adheres to two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant facts related to ion substitution and its effect on crystal strength.)

Calcium or phosphate substitutions lack support from evidence regarding strengthened crystalline integrity. \boxed{A}
